commit.message:
Fix audio track button never appearing for identified titles

HasAudioTracks() required audio tracks to already have non-empty titles,
creating a chicken-and-egg problem: the button to label tracks was hidden
until tracks were already labeled.

Add HasAudioSegments() that checks if a title has audio-type segments,
and use it with IsIdentified() as the button visibility condition. This
follows the same pattern as the chapter button (IsIdentified && ChapterCount > 1).

HasAudioTracks() is preserved for icon/tooltip logic to distinguish
labeled vs unlabeled state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix comment column not showing for disc logs with JavaComment

The commentColumnVisible flag was only set inside the loop that iterates
over already-identified disc items. If no items had been identified yet
(or none matched), the flag stayed false even when the parsed log titles
contained JavaComment values.

Move the visibility check to scan all titles from the parsed log data
immediately after loading, independent of the identification loop.

commit.message:
Rebuild all: updated RADEON.EXE (OpenWatcom)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

RADEON: seed XOR-shift RNG from RDTSC instead of BDA timer

demo_flood() was reading the BIOS Data Area tick counter at physical
0x46C purely to seed the xorshift RNG.  RDTSC is already calibrated
and available at that point — use it directly.  Removes the last
__djgpp_conventional_base BDA-access workaround from RADEON.C.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch PLASMA to PMODETSR + fix RADEON BDA access and PMI fallback

PLASMA/build-djgpp.sh:
- Switch DPMI server from CWSDPR0 to PMODETSR.EXE (PMODE/DJ 1.3 TSR).
  Under CWSDPR0 client selectors are LDT-based; DPMI 0008h (Set Segment
  Limit) in PMODE/DJ only accepts GDT selectors (RPL=0, TI=0), so it
  rejects the LDT CS and expand_cs_to_4gb() always fell back and failed.
  Under PMODETSR all descriptors are GDT with RPL=0, so DPMI 0008h on CS
  succeeds → expand_cs_to_4gb() returns 1 → VBE3 PMI enabled.
  Direct PMI call (near call to BIOS ROM via 4GB CS) replaces INT 10h
  page-flip, eliminating real-mode round-trip overhead: ~100 MB/s gain.

PLASMA/PLASMA.C:
- When expand_cs_to_4gb() returns 0 (both DPMI 0008h and direct LDT/GDT
  patching fail), set g_no_pmi=1 so the demo falls back to VBE INT 10h
  instead of calling PMI at an address above CS.limit and #GP-ing.

PLASMA/PMODETSR.EXE:
- Add PMODE/DJ 1.3 TSR binary (from v2misc/pmode13b.zip on USB).
  Copyright (C) 1996 CW Sandmann / Thomas Pytel / Matthias Grimrath.

RADEON/RADEON.C:
- demo_flood() line 529: fix raw BDA timer read.  Under DJGPP DS.base=
  0x400000, so *(unsigned long*)0x46CUL maps to physical 0x40046C (inside
  the program image), not the BIOS Data Area at 0x46C.  Add
  __djgpp_conventional_base under #ifdef __DJGPP__.

RADEON/RADEONHW.C:
- dosbuf(), find_mode(), get_font(): all returned raw real-mode physical
  addresses without adding __djgpp_conventional_base, causing wrong memory
  accesses under DJGPP.  Fixed with #ifdef __DJGPP__ guards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Added configs to plugins  (#222)

* Disable class debug print and refactor Lua init

Comment out a noisy Con_Printf in OBJ_RegisterClass to silence debug output. In orcalib.c, simplify load_plugins by removing the temporary `f` variable and some commented-out require logic; build the dofile path and log plugin names using inline lua_tostring calls. Refactor luaopen_orca initialization to set module fields (version, build, find_metatable, async, init) and create `theme` and `config` tables via inline push/set operations; remove the previous LOADERS/table setup and commented-out plugins/config duplication.

* WIP moving initialization to Lua

* Getting new project loading to work

* Make Lua bindings tolerant and improve init

Relax strict Lua checks across bindings so missing/optional table fields no longer crash: replaced luaL_check* / luaX_check* usages with luaL_optnumber, luaL_optstring and lua_type checks and default zero-initializations for many struct fields. Changes touch SpriteKit, UIKit, core exports, filesystem bindings and other generated C bindings to safely handle absent or differently-typed Lua fields.

Also update sample and core initialization: samples/Example/package.lua renamed/adjusted (Name, WindowWidth, WindowHeight) and SystemMessages format; share/orca/core2/init.lua now initializes renderer/backend, requires orca.UIKit, loads the startup screen and adds a core.run loop to dispatch backend messages. Minor logging string quoting tweaks included.

* Add orca.system target and refactor backend

Integrate liborca.system into the Xcode project (framework entries, embed & codesign, target dependencies) and remove references to the old orca.backend target. Move/rename source files from source/backend/{backend.c,queue.c} to source/sysutil/, and update PBX groups/build phases accordingly. Update runtime Lua code: samples/Example/package.lua registers orca.UIKit as an EnginePlugin; share/orca/core2/init.lua now uses orca.system (system.getMessage/system.dispatchMessage), adds handling for WindowClosed/KeyDown('q')/RequestReload, and removes require "orca.backend". Overall this switches message handling to the new system module and cleans up backend-related project entries.

* Remove FS_RegisterObject; fold backend into system

Remove the deprecated FS_RegisterObject API and its usage across the codebase: header and XML documentation entries removed, implementation deleted from filesystem.c, Lua binding removed from filesystem_export.c, and calls removed from plugins/file-xml.lua and plugins/DarkReign/DarkReign.c.

Also consolidate backend event/message functionality into the system module: remove registration of the orca.backend module in orcalib, drop backend-specific exports/implementation, and add getMessage/dispatchMessage and Event metatable setup to the system module (w_system.c) by declaring the relevant functions and exposing them via lib_system. Additionally, comment out an early exit in main (orca.c) that prematurely terminated execution during tests.

* Improve project loading and filesystem handling

Delete unused sample package, add safer project/screen/editor loading and file-tracking, and fix path/file handling in C code.

- Removed samples/Example/package_.xml.
- share/orca/core2/init.lua: reorder plugin loading, add core.load_screen and core.load_editor helpers, call core.load_editor, and enable filesystem.trackChangedFiles() in the run loop.
- source/filesystem/Directory.c: guard lua_loadfile_with_env with a FILE open/close wrapper.
- source/filesystem/filesystem.c: build the joined path into a buffer and pass it to FS_LoadBundle (avoid temporary/stack misuse).
- source/orca.c: print bootstrap errors with newline and read bootstrap return value (string) into szProject when provided.

These changes improve robustness around loading projects, screens and editor integration, and make file/path handling safer.

* Load plugin configs, add theme sample, init fixes

Add a Tailwind-style sample theme (samples/Example/config/tailwind.lua).

Refactor core initialization (share/orca/core2/init.lua): initialize core.configs, load the startup screen into core.screen, change editor loading to use core.screen (load_editor no longer takes args), and add core.load_plugin_config to load per-plugin config files from each workspace package. Call load_plugin_config after successfully loading each plugin.

Adjust project package loading (source/filesystem/Directory.c) to attempt loading package.lua earlier during project load (adds an existence-guarded lua_loadfile_with_env call).

* Export readTextFile API and update callers

Add a FS_ReadTextFile binding and rename the Lua API from read_file to readTextFile, including header and XML docs. Update native implementation to return exact byte lengths (lua_pushlstring) and export the new function in luaopen_orca_filesystem. Update Lua callers to use filesystem.readTextFile (samples/Banking Application, core2 init, and file-css plugin) and change core config loading to read file contents and use load() on the string instead of loadfile. Also remove/disable a debug print and adjust export table entries accordingly.

commit.message:
Fix XamlMessageBox STA Threading Crash from Background Threads (#2400)

* Fix XamlMessageBox STA threading crash from background threads

XamlMessageBox.Show() now auto-dispatches to the UI thread when called
from a background thread, matching the old System.Windows.MessageBox
behavior. This fixes the SecurityCheck delegate crash during symbol
resolution, where the delegate is invoked on Task.Run threads.

The fix checks Application.Current.Dispatcher.CheckAccess() and uses
synchronous Dispatcher.Invoke to marshal the call, preserving the
return value for callers. Existing UI-thread call sites are unaffected
(CheckAccess returns true, no dispatch needed).

Fixes #2300

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* review-loop iteration 1: prefer owner dispatcher, guard Application singleton, guard RegisterClassHandler

- XamlMessageBox.cs: Use owner?.Dispatcher ?? Application.Current?.Dispatcher
  so the dispatch targets the correct UI thread when an owner window is provided.
  Simplify with Dispatcher.Invoke(Func<T>) to return result directly.
- XamlMessageBoxTests.cs: Guard Application creation with Application.Current ??
  to prevent InvalidOperationException in shared AppDomains.
- XamlMessageBoxTests.cs: Add static guard for RegisterClassHandler (permanent,
  AppDomain-wide registration) and use XamlMBTest_ prefix for unique captions.
- XamlMessageBoxTests.cs: Use indexer assignment for theme resources (idempotent).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* review-loop iteration 2: use current STA dispatcher instead of app.Dispatcher in test

Replace app.Dispatcher.BeginInvoke with Dispatcher.CurrentDispatcher.BeginInvoke
to ensure work is queued to the dispatcher being pumped by Dispatcher.Run().
If Application.Current was reused from a prior test on a different thread,
app.Dispatcher would target that thread's dispatcher, causing the test to hang.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: start idle standby timer on daemon connect without audio

The idle disconnect timer only started on a streaming→idle transition
(audio_streaming: True→False). If a device connected but never played
audio, the timer never started and the speaker stayed connected
indefinitely.

Now also start the timer when server_connected becomes True while
audio_streaming is false and the device is not already in standby.

Reported by community user on HA forum (v2.50.3).

* Fix CFG predict_step signature conflict with PyTorch Lightning

Both CFG models had predict_step(self, batch, guidance_scale=None) which
caused PyTorch Lightning to pass batch_idx as guidance_scale, silently
overriding the guidance scale set on the model. This resulted in:
- Wrong guidance scales being used during evaluation
- Nearly identical scores across different guidance strengths
- Worse scores compared to non-CFG models

Fix: Changed signature to predict_step(self, batch, batch_idx=None) and
read guidance_scale from self.guidance_scale attribute instead.

* Add permutation_importance_drn.py for DRN/EMOS models with copula postprocessing

DRN/EMOS models output distribution parameters (mu, sigma) rather than
samples directly. This script extends permutation importance to handle
the extra copula step:

1. Load model + data, run baseline forward pass
2. Convert distributional params to forecast samples via ECC or GCA
3. Compute baseline energy score on the copula samples
4. For each channel: inject PermuteChannel → forward pass → copula → ES
5. Record (ES_permuted - ES_baseline) / ES_baseline

CLI: python -m genpp.eval.permutation_importance_drn \
       --run-path feik/genpp/m5y9kwlh --split val --copula ecc -v

Reuses helpers from copulas_eval.py (stack_predictions,
predictions_to_dataarray, do_ecc, do_gca) and permutation_importance.py
(_build_x_transform, _get_channel_info, parse_device, log_msg).

Co-authored-by: m-j-w-f <98650214+m-j-w-f@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add parallel launcher for DRN permutation importance

Discovers all input channels from cached metadata, launches one
permutation_importance_drn.py subprocess per channel in parallel.
Each subprocess writes to a unique temp CSV to avoid write collisions.
Results are merged into a single sorted output file once all finish.

CLI: python -m genpp.eval.launch_permutation_importance_drn \
       --run-path feik/genpp/m5y9kwlh --max-parallel 4 --device 0 -v

Features:
- --max-parallel flag to limit concurrent subprocesses
- --channels to subset channels (forwarded to each subprocess)
- Per-channel temp files prevent write collisions
- Merged CSV sorted by importance descending

* Fix NaN in spatial normalization: clamp variance and std in _compute_tensor_stats

Two sources of NaN in spatial mode:
1. Negative variance from E[x²]-E[x]² numerical cancellation → sqrt(negative) = NaN
2. Zero std at constant grid cells → division by zero = Inf/NaN in __getitem__

Fix: clamp variance to min=0 before sqrt, clamp std to min=1e-6 in spatial mode.
This prevents NaN in norm_stats which was propagating to all_vars_mean and all_vars_std.

Add GA4 form_submission event for Google Ads conversion tracking

Both email signup and contact forms now fire a 'form_submission' custom
event with form_id, form_name, and form_destination parameters on
successful submission. Also kept generate_lead recommended event with
GA4-native parameters (replaced legacy event_category/event_label).

The ads person can now mark 'form_submission' as a conversion event in
GA4 Admin > Events, then import it into Google Ads for conversion tracking.

commit.message:
Remove platform prefixes from file names in platform source sets

Rename files in androidMain, iosMain, and jvmMain to remove redundant
Android/Ios/Jvm prefixes since the directory already scopes the platform.

File renames:
- AndroidCryptoProvider.kt → CryptoProvider.kt (all three source sets)
- AndroidBatteryMonitor.kt → BatteryMonitor.kt (all three source sets)
- AndroidSecureStorage.kt → SecureStorage.kt (android, ios)
- AndroidBleTransport.kt → BleTransport.kt (android, ios)
- AndroidBleConstants.kt → BleConstants.kt (android only)
- AndroidPlatformLock.kt → PlatformLock.kt (all three source sets)
- AndroidTime.kt → Time.kt (all three source sets)
- IosStatePreservation.kt → StatePreservation.kt (ios only)

Class/object renames (where no interface name collision):
- object AndroidBleConstants → BleConstants
- class IosStatePreservation → StatePreservation

Implementation class names (AndroidBatteryMonitor, IosBleTransport, etc.)
are kept as-is to avoid redeclaration conflicts with their same-named
commonMain interfaces.

Added @file:JvmName annotations to platform files whose names now match
commonMain files, preventing duplicate JVM facade class generation.

commit.message:
Fix Sonar coverage reporting: switch from LCOV to Generic Coverage XML (#19)

* Initial plan

* Fix coverage reporting: convert LCOV to Sonar Generic Coverage XML

The CFamily plugin 6.79.0 silently ignores sonar.cfamily.coverage.reportPaths
(no log output confirming the file is read). Switch to the Sonar Generic Coverage
XML format via sonar.coverageReportPaths, which uses a dedicated sensor that
reliably processes coverage for any language including C++.

Changes:
- scripts/generate_coverage.py: add lcov_to_sonar_xml() that converts the LCOV
  report to Sonar Generic Coverage XML with project-relative file paths
- sonar-project.properties: replace cfamily-specific property with the standard
  sonar.coverageReportPaths pointing to the XML file
- .github/workflows/test.yml: upload both coverage files as artifacts and pass
  the XML path to the SonarQube scan action
